sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: VictoryShares US 500 Volatility Wtd ETF (NASDAQ: CFA) - Share Price
feed.summary: This article provides the current and historical share price data for the VictoryShares US 500 Volatility Wtd ETF (NASDAQ: CFA). The current share price is $96.05, representing a 0.39% increase today and a -0.10% movement over the last seven days. Detailed performance figures are presented for the current year, last year, this financial year, and last financial year, along with a historical daily share price chart.

feed.title: Technical Analysis of Simplify High Yield ETF (AMEX:CDX)
feed.summary: This article provides a technical analysis of the Simplify High Yield ETF (AMEX:CDX) based on various indicators. It summarizes signals from oscillators, moving averages, and pivots, providing an overall technical rating. The analysis currently indicates a "strong sell" for CDX, emphasizing that market conditions are fluid and encouraging investors to consult multiple data sources.

feed.title: CLSK sold one of its highest proportions of mined BTC during February.
feed.summary: CleanSpark sold 97% of its February Bitcoin production, totaling 553 BTC for $36.65 million, to finance its expansion into artificial intelligence and high-performance computing data centers. This move reflects a broader trend among Bitcoin miners pivoting towards AI as they seek to fund new infrastructure development. Despite the significant sale, CleanSpark retains 13,363 BTC in its treasury and is expanding its mining platform, including a new 300 MW campus in Texas.

feed.title: Compass Therapeutics Reports 2025 Financial Results and Provides Corporate Update
feed.summary: Compass Therapeutics has released its full 2025 financial results, highlighting significant progress in its clinical-stage pipeline. The company reported $209 million in cash and marketable securities, providing a runway into 2028. Key updates include reaching the event threshold for its tovecimig study in biliary tract cancer, expanding cohorts for CTX-8371 in TNBC and NSCLC, and FDA clearance for CTX-10726, with Phase 1 studies commencing.

feed.title: UBS Maintains Buy Rating for ConocoPhillips (COP), Raises Price Target to 144.00 USD | COP Stock News
feed.summary: UBS has re-affirmed its Buy rating for ConocoPhillips (COP) and increased its price target from $130.00 to $144.00 USD, reflecting a 10.77% rise. This decision, along with recent similar upgrades from other firms, indicates a positive outlook from analysts on the company's performance, despite GuruFocus's own estimated fair value suggesting a downside from the current price. ConocoPhillips is a US-based independent exploration and production firm with global operations.

feed.title: Westrock Coffee CEO Scott Ford Named to UA Board of Trustees
feed.summary: Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders has appointed Scott Ford, CEO and co-founder of Westrock Coffee, to the University of Arkansas board of trustees. Ford replaces Morril Harriman and his term will last until March 1, 2034. Sanders highlighted Ford's entrepreneurial success and leadership, stating he will be an invaluable member of the board.

feed.title: Idexx Launch of InVue Provides Path to Recurring Revenue
feed.summary: Idexx Laboratories is poised for recurring revenue growth following the launch of its InVue product, further strengthening its position in the veterinary diagnostic lab market. The company benefits from increased pet ownership and owners' willingness to spend more on pet care. This aligns with a December 2025 report highlighting the InVue Cellular Analyzer's potential impact.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway has no immediate plans on its Kraft Heinz stake - report (BRK.B:NYSE)
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway currently has no plans to alter its stake in Kraft Heinz, according to CEO Greg Abel. This decision comes after Kraft Heinz canceled its previous plan to split into two separate entities. The statement was made in a televised interview on Thursday.

feed.title: International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) Partners With Deepgram to Support Its Generative AI Solution
feed.summary: IBM has partnered with Deepgram to integrate its speech-to-text and text-to-speech technologies into IBM's Watsonx Orchestrate generative AI solution. This collaboration aims to automate operations and meet the demand for conversational AI, supporting various dialects and regional accents. Additionally, IBM secured a $112 million contract from the Defense Commissary Agency to modernize electronic shelf label systems.

feed.title: TAPinto Announces Johnson & Johnson, PSE&G as New Signature Marketing Partners
feed.summary: TAPinto has announced that Johnson & Johnson and PSE&G have joined as new Signature Marketing Partners. This partnership signifies a collaboration between the local news network and these major corporations to enhance marketing efforts. The announcement highlights TAPinto's growing influence and its ability to attract significant partners.

feed.title: Fed board ends 2018 enforcement action against Wells Fargo (WFC:NYSE)
feed.summary: The Federal Reserve Board has terminated a 2018 consent order against Wells Fargo. This enforcement action had required the bank to improve its governance and risk management programs and complete two third-party reviews. The termination signifies that Wells Fargo has met these requirements.

feed.title: Analysts Reduce PT on EPAM Systems (EPAM) While Keeping Positive Outlook
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities and TD Cowen have both trimmed their price targets for EPAM Systems (EPAM) due to its 2026 organic growth guidance falling short of expectations, particularly from a large Neoris customer. Despite the reductions, both firms maintain positive outlooks and 'Outperform' or 'Buy' ratings, citing strong underlying business momentum, improving pricing, AI-driven demand, margin expansion, and balance sheet strength. Analysts see the recent selloff as a buying opportunity, with an average price target indicating significant upside potential.

feed.title: Barco Joins Coca-Cola as Co-Presenting Sponsor of CinemaCon 2026
feed.summary: Barco has joined The Coca-Cola Company as a co-presenting sponsor for CinemaCon 2026, marking the first time the global theatrical convention will have two presenting partners. This partnership highlights Barco's commitment to the cinema industry, showcasing its laser projection technology and emphasizing collaboration among filmmakers, studios, exhibitors, and technology partners. CinemaCon 2026, organized by Cinema United, will take place from April 13-16 at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas.

feed.title: Stratasys acquires Arevo 3D printing assets and IP
feed.summary: Stratasys has acquired the technology portfolio and intellectual property (IP) of carbon fiber 3D printing firm Arevo, following an asset auction. Arevo, known for its AQUA 3D printers and later its SuperStrata bikes, ceased operations last year despite significant funding. Stratasys plans to leverage this IP, which includes carbon fiber 3D printing and AI-powered monitoring, to enhance its FDM technology, improve part isotropy, and boost 3D printer throughput for manufacturing applications.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Opens Bay Area Cryo-EM Drug Discovery Center to Accelerate Breakthrough Therapies
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched its new Cryo-Electron Microscopy (cryo-EM) Drug Discovery Center (CDDC) in South San Francisco to accelerate structural insights and drug development. This center offers pharmaceutical and biotechnology innovators direct access to advanced cryo-EM technologies and expertise, aiming to lower adoption barriers and speed up the delivery of life-saving medicines. The CDDC builds on a decade of successful collaboration in the UK and seeks to replicate that model in the North American market.

feed.title: Cracker Barrel sees slow improvement despite continued traffic slump in Q2
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel is showing slow signs of improvement despite an ongoing traffic slump in Q2, reporting a 7.1% decline in same-store sales but improved traffic and guest scores in January. The company is focusing on heritage, food, and value perception improvements after abandoning controversial brand modernization plans. Strategies include reintroducing menu favorites, adding more breakfast options, implementing barbell pricing, and undertaking cost-saving measures.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Recently Broke Out Above the 20-Day Moving Average
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has shown a short-term bullish trend and could be a good stock pick, having recently broken out above its 20-day moving average. The company has gained 20.2% in the past four weeks, holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), and has seen positive earnings estimate revisions. Investors should monitor AMAT for potential further gains due to this combination of technical strength and favorable estimates.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts See a 32.21% Upside in Autodesk (ADSK): Can the Stock Really Move This High?
feed.summary: Autodesk (ADSK) shares have recently gained 6%, and Wall Street analysts project a potential upside of 32.2% with a mean price target of $340.96. While analysts' price targets can be overly optimistic, a low standard deviation indicates agreement among them. The stock also holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), driven by a positive trend in earnings estimate revisions, suggesting a legitimate reason for expected upside despite the skepticism around price targets alone.

feed.title: New DAT iPhone widget puts top-paying freight loads on your home screen
feed.summary: DAT Freight & Analytics has launched the DAT One iOS widget for iPhone, allowing owner-operators to view the top three load matches directly on their home screen. This widget automatically refreshes every five minutes using saved search preferences and facilitates one-tap contact with brokers, aiming to expedite the load finding process for carriers. The release aligns with Roper Technologies' strategy of incremental digital enhancements across its portfolio.

feed.title: As Last Mile Delivery Shifts Toward Orchestration and AI, OneRail Recognized in the Gartner® Market Guide
feed.summary: OneRail, a leading final mile delivery orchestration platform, has been recognized in the 2024 Gartner Market Guide for Last-Mile Delivery Technology. The report emphasizes the growing trend of leveraging AI for advanced orchestration, highlighting OneRail's capabilities in unifying disparate systems, optimizing delivery decisions, and providing end-to-end visibility. This recognition underscores OneRail's role in helping enterprises achieve greater efficiency, customer satisfaction, and scalability in their last-mile operations.

feed.title: Netflix just acquired an AI-tech filmmaking company
feed.summary: Netflix has acquired InterPositive, an AI-tech filmmaking company founded by Ben Affleck. This acquisition aims to bolster Netflix's in-house AI filmmaking capabilities, aligning with its mission to use technology to enhance creative choices for storytellers. While the financial terms were not disclosed, this move positions Netflix at the forefront of AI innovation in the film and TV industry, potentially impacting industry jobs.

feed.title: Verizon Shares Rise 19.4% in a Year: Should You Invest Now?
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) shares have risen 19.4% in the past year, outperforming its industry and peers like AT&T and T-Mobile. The company is benefiting from strong subscriber growth in its Consumer segment, driven by its convergence strategy and growth in fixed wireless access. Despite strong performance and diversification into AI-enabled telematics, Verizon faces challenges from stiff competition, a saturated wireless market, and a high debt burden, leading Zacks Investment Research to give it a "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Barrington Adjusts Price Target on Climb Global Solutions to $120 From $136, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Barrington has adjusted its price target for Climb Global Solutions (CLMB) to $120, down from $136, while retaining an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This revision comes alongside recent news regarding Climb Global Solutions, including a new partnership deal with LogicMonitor and their Q4 2025 earnings report. The company also announced the suspension of quarterly cash dividends on common stock and the acquisition of Interworks S.A.

feed.title: Alpha Buying: Following the Insiders Into a Volatile Market
feed.summary: This article discusses "Alpha Buying" by following corporate insiders' purchases during market volatility, specifically in the context of increased geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The author highlights that insider buying, especially ahead of macro shocks, signals that executives believe their companies are undervalued. Four companies—CoStar Group (CSGP), Hamilton Lane (HLNE), Vornado Realty Trust (VNO), and Claritev (CTEV)—are presented as examples where insiders bought shares before the recent market downturn, suggesting long-term value despite current fears.

feed.title: The Top 50 Originators Account for 91% of Total ACH Commercial Payments Volume, Nacha Says
feed.summary: In 2025, the Top 50 originating financial institutions processed 30.7 billion ACH payments, representing 91.4% of the total commercial payments volume, a slight decrease from 2024. Wells Fargo led originators, while Bank of America topped receivers. The growth in ACH payments is attributed to a continued shift towards electronic payments for both business and consumer transactions, including increasing adoption of same-day ACH.

feed.title: Compass Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CMPX) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.01 EPS
feed.summary: Compass Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CMPX) announced its quarterly earnings, reporting a loss of ($0.09) per share, which surpassed the consensus estimate of ($0.10) per share by $0.01. Following the announcement, the stock traded up approximately 2.5% to $5.83. The company currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $13.36, and institutional investors own 68.43% of the stock.

feed.title: Darden invests $3.5M in new Seasons 52 location in Winter Garden
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants is investing $3.5 million to open a new Seasons 52 location in Winter Garden, Florida, on the site of a former Romano's Macaroni Grill. A recently issued construction permit revealed the cost for the new restaurant. This development is part of a larger shopping center that attracts millions of visitors annually.

feed.title: Federal Reserve ends 2018 oversight order against Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company announced that the Federal Reserve Board of Governors terminated its 2018 consent order concerning governance oversight and compliance and operational

feed.title: Wells Fargo 2018 Federal Reserve Consent Order Terminated
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company announced that the Federal Reserve Board of Governors has terminated the 2018 consent order related to governance oversight and compliance and operational risk management. Wells Fargo is a leading financial services company with approximately $2.1 trillion in assets, offering a diversified range of banking, investment, and mortgage products. The company was ranked No. 33 on Fortune’s 2025 list of America’s largest corporations.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) is expected to report its earnings for the quarter ended January 2026 on March 12, with analysts anticipating a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues. The company has a Zacks Earnings ESP of +11.98% and a Zacks Rank #3, suggesting a strong likelihood of an earnings beat. Historically, Ulta has consistently surpassed EPS estimates, beating consensus in all of the last four quarters.

feed.title: Understanding institutional real estate investing
feed.summary: Institutional investors like pension funds and sovereign wealth funds are crucial capital sources for commercial real estate, attracted by diversification and solid returns. They typically allocate 8% to 15% of their investments to real estate, often providing capital to developers and funds rather than investing directly. Attracting this capital requires extensive due diligence, a clear strategy, a strong track record, and sophisticated operations.

feed.title: CVS Health partners with Google; launching health tech subsidiary
feed.summary: CVS Health is partnering with Google Cloud to launch Health100, a new health technology services subsidiary. This collaboration aims to revolutionize healthcare experiences, enhance consumer engagement, and support better health outcomes by leveraging Google's AI technologies, including Gemini models. Health100 will provide an integrated platform designed to offer real-time omnichannel support and connect consumers with pharmacist-led care management, with an initial launch planned for 2026.

feed.title: TSMC, Samsung Lead Recovery In Asia's Emerging Markets With AI And Chip Sector Growth
feed.summary: Emerging markets, particularly in Asia, are recovering from 2022 losses, driven by tech giants like TSMC and Samsung, despite global headwinds. The recovery is fueled by demand in the AI and semiconductor sectors, offering attractive valuations compared to developed markets. While China risk remains, alternative ETFs excluding China provide options for investors.

feed.title: Vertex Pharmaceuticals to Present Data on JOURNAVX® for Pain Management After Aesthetic and Reconstructive Surgeries
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals announced that it will present new data on its product JOURNAVX® for effective pain management following aesthetic and reconstructive procedures. The study showed significant improvements in patient-reported outcomes for pain when using JOURNAVX®. This presentation is part of the company's efforts to expand its portfolio in therapeutic solutions for surgical recovery.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Announces 2027 Earnings Call Schedule
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp has announced its schedule for 2027 quarterly earnings conference calls, which will take place on April 15, July 15, October 14, and January 15. These calls are important for investors and analysts to gain insights into the company's financial health and the broader financial sector. The company will provide access to these calls via its website and phone.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern Trades at Premium Valuation: How to Play the Stock?
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NSC) is currently trading at a premium valuation with a forward 12-month price-to-sales ratio of 25.17X, which is above its industry average. Despite headwinds like macroeconomic concerns, a tough freight environment, and railroad network issues, the company benefits from e-commerce growth, its Precision Scheduled Railroading (PSR) operating plan, and a strong balance sheet. Investors are advised to wait for a better entry point or stay invested if they already own the stock, as the company holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Con Edison Is Up 13% in 2026: Here’s Why Analysts Still See 47% Upside
feed.summary: Con Edison (ED) is up 13% in 2026, driven by a $37.7 billion capital investment plan through 2030 and a recently approved three-year rate case. While the stock has increased, TIKR's valuation model suggests a 46.9% total return by 2030, indicating the market may be underpricing the utility's earnings durability and growth story. Analysts maintain a mean price target of $109.6, but some see a bull case of $130 (and a TIKR model target of $165.23) given the company's dividend history and regulatory protections.

feed.title: Honeywell takes next step in aerospace spinoff, offers more details
feed.summary: Honeywell has filed a Form 10 registration statement, providing more details about the upcoming spinoff of its aerospace division. This move is a significant step in the regulatory process, with the offshoot expected to become a publicly traded company on Nasdaq under the ticker "HONA" in the third quarter of 2026. Honeywell aims to focus on its core industrial and automation segments, while the new aerospace entity, which generated $17.4 billion in sales in 2025, will concentrate on efficiency, safety, and connectivity in commercial and defense aviation.

feed.title: Broadridge selects Weinberg
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions has appointed Allen Weinberg as chief growth and strategy officer. In this new role, Weinberg will focus on accelerating growth, enhancing profitability, and strengthening competitive differentiation, reporting directly to CEO Tim Gokey. Weinberg previously spent 32 years at McKinsey & Company, leading their North American Banking Tech and Ops practice.

feed.title: Adobe Systems (ADBE) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Adobe Systems (ADBE) is projected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended February 2026, with an expected EPS of $5.88 and revenues of $6.28 billion. Despite a positive Earnings ESP of +0.04%, the company's Zacks Rank #4 makes it difficult to definitively predict an earnings beat, though Adobe has consistently surpassed EPS estimates in the past four quarters. Investors are advised to consider other factors alongside these predictions for their investment decisions.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for Skyworks Solutions
feed.summary: This article analyzes Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ: SWKS) P/E ratio in comparison to its industry peers. Currently trading at $55.96, the stock has seen a 0.66% increase today but a significant dip over the past month and year. The lower P/E ratio relative to the Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment industry suggests the stock might be undervalued, but also cautions that a low P/E doesn't guarantee future performance and should be considered alongside other financial metrics and market trends.

feed.title: Nvidia investing $2B in Coherent
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. is investing $2 billion in Saxonburg-based Coherent Corp., a manufacturer of semiconductors and lasers, as part of a larger $4 billion investment in photonic technology companies. This investment also includes a nonexclusive agreement where Nvidia will purchase advanced laser and optical products from Coherent to support R&D and expand Coherent’s U.S. manufacturing. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that this partnership aims to pioneer next-generation silicon photonics for AI infrastructure, emphasizing speed and energy efficiency.

feed.title: Canadian Natural Resources reports $5.3B Q4 profit, raises quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Canadian Natural Resources Ltd. reported a fourth-quarter profit of $5.30 billion, significantly up from $1.14 billion a year earlier. Following this strong performance, the company announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to 62.5 cents per share from 58.75 cents. Production also rose, totaling 1,658,681 barrels of oil equivalent per day before royalties.

feed.title: IDEX Stock Exhibits Strong Prospects Despite Persisting Headwinds
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation (IEX) is experiencing strong performance in its Fluid & Metering Technologies (FMT) and Health & Science Technologies (HST) segments, driven by increased demand and strategic acquisitions. Despite headwinds in its Fire & Safety/Diversified Products (FSDP) segment and foreign currency risks, the company continues to reward shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. IDEX holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) and its shares have outperformed the industry in the past three months.

feed.title: Canadian Natural Resources Limited Announces Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Canadian Natural Resources Limited announced a 6.4% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.625 per common share, marking its 26th consecutive year of dividend increases. The dividend is payable on April 7, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 20, 2026. This increase reflects the company's confidence in its sustainable business model, strong balance sheet, and diverse asset base.

feed.title: Alphabet Stock Edges Lower in Early Trading as AI Capex Concerns Weigh Amid Geopolitical Volatility
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. shares saw a modest dip in early trading, affected by broader market caution stemming from rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions, in addition to investor concerns over the company's substantial 2026 capital expenditure plans for AI infrastructure. The company plans to spend between $175 billion and $185 billion, primarily on servers, data centers, and networking to support its AI initiatives like Gemini, doubling 2025's estimated spending. While analysts view this as a strategic bet on AI dominance, there are warnings about potential near-term pressure on free cash flow and margins, despite the long-term optimism from Wall Street, which maintains "Strong Buy" ratings.

feed.title: Dow jumps as analysts turn more constructive amid oil-driven sentiment shift
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (DOW) experienced a 6.6% increase in its stock price, driven by renewed analyst optimism. This shift includes ratings upgrades and higher price targets from firms like BMO Capital Markets and KeyBanc, partly influenced by rising oil prices and geopolitical factors favoring U.S.-based chemical producers. The article also highlights significant institutional investor activity and congressional trading in DOW stock.

feed.title: Coherent Corp. (COHR) Gains on AI Data Center Networking Demand and Margin Expansion
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. (COHR) saw its shares rise due to increased demand for AI data center networking and improved profit margins, as highlighted in Invesco Ltd's Small Cap Value Fund's Q4 2025 investor letter. The Invesco fund outperformed its benchmark, with strong performance in the Information Technology sector. Despite Coherent's gains, the fund suggested that other AI stocks might offer even greater short-term returns.

feed.title: Booking Holdings jumps as stock-split and shareholder-return headlines keep buyers engaged
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) shares rose 6.9% following the announcement of a 25-for-1 forward stock split and an increased quarterly dividend of $10.50 per share. These actions, coupled with bullish analyst ratings and strong 2026 growth targets, are attracting investor demand despite no change in underlying fundamentals. The stock split is set for April 2, 2026, and the dividend is payable March 31, 2026.

feed.title: Bridgewater Bancshares (NASDAQ: BWB) launches $50M at-the-market stock offering
feed.summary: Bridgewater Bancshares, Inc. (NASDAQ: BWB) has announced an at-the-market (ATM) stock offering of up to $50 million through an Equity Distribution Agreement with Piper Sandler & Co. This allows the company to periodically issue common stock for general corporate purposes, including investments, working capital, capital expenditures, stock repurchases, debt repayment, or financing acquisitions. Piper Sandler will receive a 2.5% commission on gross sales, and the company is not obligated to sell any shares.
